10.1.title: भूय एव — the renewed invitation to listen
10.1.body: Krishna opens with भूय एव ('once again') and the लोट् imperative शृणु ('listen!'), framing chapter 10 as a deliberate second pass over material already touched. The motive is given in the तृतीया phrase हितकाम्यया and the dative प्रीयमाणाय — He speaks परमं वचः not because Arjuna asked, but because Arjuna is loved.

10.1.title: वक्ष्यामि — the future tense that frames the chapter
10.1.body: वक्ष्यामि (√वच्, लृट्, उत्तम एकवचन) is spotted by the -ष्य- infix and locks the chapter's structure: everything that follows is promised speech. यत्...वक्ष्यामि is a relative clause whose antecedent is परमं वचः — the 'supreme utterance' that the vibhūti enumeration will turn out to be.

10.2.title: न विदुः — even gods don't know His origin
10.2.body: विदुः is √विद्, लट्, प्रथम बहुवचन ('they know'), negated twice — सुरगणाः and महर्षयः are both subjects in प्रथमा बहुवचन. The reason follows with हि: अहम् आदिः 'I am the source' — they cannot know His प्रभव because they themselves originate downstream of Him.

10.2.title: आदिः — the षष्ठी sweep of देवानां महर्षीणां
10.2.body: देवानां and महर्षीणां are both षष्ठी बहुवचन ('of gods, of great seers'), gathered under one predicate आदिः. सर्वशः ('in every way') closes the door on partial exceptions — no class of divine being escapes the claim.

10.3.title: अजम् अनादिं — the two negations that define Him
10.3.body: मां is qualified by three द्वितीया एकवचन adjectives: अजम् ('unborn'), अनादिं ('beginningless'), and लोकमहेश्वरम् ('great lord of worlds'). The first two are negative compounds (अ-ज, अन्-आदि) — Krishna is defined by what He is not subject to, and the knower of this becomes असम्मूढः.

10.3.title: प्रमुच्यते — the कर्मवाच्य of liberation
10.3.body: प्रमुच्यते is passive (कर्मवाच्य) — 'he is released' — with the agent of release suppressed; the तृतीया सर्वपापैः marks what he is released from. The construction is deliberate: liberation happens to the knower, it is not something he does.

10.4.title: Thirteen dispositions in mixed gender प्रथमा
10.4.body: बुद्धिः ज्ञानम् असम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः सुखं दुःखं भवः अभावः भयं अभयम् — a long parallel list of qualities in प्रथमा एकवचन, spanning all three genders (स्त्री.: बुद्धिः, क्षमा; न.: ज्ञानम्, सत्यम्, सुखम्, दुःखम्, भयम्, अभयम्; पु.: असम्मोहः, दमः, शमः, भवः, अभावः). They are gathered as subjects whose predicate ('arise from Me') will land only in verse 5. The grammar holds its breath across the verse boundary.

10.4.title: Pairs of opposites under one source
10.4.body: The list deliberately pairs opposites: सुखं/दुःखं, भवः/अभावः, भयं/अभयम् — Krishna refuses the dualistic instinct to assign 'good' qualities to Himself and 'bad' to something else. एव च after अभयम् binds the contrastive pair into a single grammatical unit.

10.5.title: मत्तः एव — the पञ्चमी that completes verse 4
10.5.body: The finite verb finally arrives: भवन्ति (√भू, लट्, प्रथम बहुवचन). Subject is भावाः ('dispositions') with पृथग्विधाः ('of various kinds') and the षष्ठी qualifier भूतानाम् ('of beings'). The crucial word is the पञ्चमी मत्तः ('from Me') intensified by एव — these states arise from no other source.

10.5.title: Even अहिंसा and यशः traced upstream
10.5.body: The list extends: अहिंसा, समता, तुष्टिः, तपः, दानं, यशः, अयशः. यशः/अयशः again pair opposites — fame and infamy alike originate in Him. सामानाधिकरण्य holds: पृथग्विधाः agrees with भावाः in प्रथमा बहुवचन पुल्लिंग.

10.6.title: मानसाः जाताः — born of His mind
10.6.body: मद्भावाः ('having My nature') and मानसाः जाताः ('mind-born') describe the seven seers and four Manus. जाताः is a past passive participle agreeing with the प्रथमा बहुवचन subjects — born, but born from Krishna's mind, not from biological lineage. येषाम् (षष्ठी) makes them the ancestors of इमाः प्रजाः.

10.6.title: The cosmic genealogy in seven and four
10.6.body: सप्त and चत्वारः are numerical adjectives in प्रथमा agreeing with महर्षयः and मनवः. The verse compresses entire purāṇic cosmogony into one line: every human लोक descends from beings who themselves descend from Krishna's mind.

10.7.title: योगेन युज्यते — instrumental of the very thing yoked
10.7.body: युज्यते is passive (√युज्, लट्, प्रथम एकवचन) — 'he is yoked'. The तृतीया अविकम्पेन योगेन ('by unwavering yoga') is the means. एताम् विभूतिं योगं च are द्वितीया objects of वेत्ति, and तत्त्वतः ('truly, in essence') specifies how the knowing must happen.

10.7.title: न अत्र संशयः — the formulaic seal
10.7.body: नात्र संशयः ('there is no doubt about this') is Krishna's stock guarantor across the Gītā. Grammatically it is an elliptical clause with अस्ति suppressed; pragmatically it marks the verse as a promise rather than a description.

10.8.title: मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते — the cosmic flow
10.8.body: प्रवर्तते (√वृत् with प्र, लट् आत्मनेपद, प्रथम एकवचन) is the verb of unfolding; subject सर्वं ('all') is नपुंसकलिंग एकवचन; मत्तः is पञ्चमी ('from Me'). इति मत्वा ('having thought thus') uses the absolutive of √मन्, framing the bhakta's bhajana as a consequence of insight.

10.8.title: बुधाः भावसमन्विताः — the wise, infused with bhāva
10.8.body: Subject of भजन्ते is बुधाः ('the wise', प्रथमा बहुवचन), qualified by भावसमन्विताः — a bahuvrīhi describing those whose bhāva is fully integrated. भजन्ते is आत्मनेपद, marking devotion as something done for and within the self.

10.9.title: Four participles painting the bhakta's life
10.9.body: मच्चित्ताः, मद्गतप्राणाः, बोधयन्तः, कथयन्तश्च — the first two are bahuvrīhi compounds, the latter two are present active participles (शतृ) in प्रथमा बहुवचन. They surround the two finite verbs तुष्यन्ति (√तुष्) and रमन्ति (√रम्), both लट् प्रथम बहुवचन.

10.9.title: तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च — two verbs, one life
10.9.body: The pairing 'they are satisfied and they delight' captures bhakti as both fulfillment (तुष्यन्ति, √तुष्, लट्, प्रथम बहुवचन) and play (रमन्ति, √रम्, लट् आत्मनेपद-flavoured form, प्रथम बहुवचन). परस्परम् ('mutually') tells us this is a communal phenomenon — the bhakta is plural here, not solitary.

10.10.title: ददामि बुद्धियोगं — the gift Krishna gives
10.10.body: ददामि is √दा, लट्, उत्तम एकवचन ('I give'). The recipients are तेषाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन) further specified by two genitive participles सततयुक्तानां ('of those constantly yoked') and भजताम् ('of those worshipping'). The gift is बुद्धियोगम् (द्वितीया).

10.10.title: येन उपयान्ति — instrumental of approach
10.10.body: येन मामुपयान्ति is a relative clause: येन is तृतीया ('by which'), उपयान्ति (√या with उप, लट्, प्रथम बहुवचन). The buddhi-yoga is given as instrument; the bhaktas use it to come to Him. प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ('preceded by love') is the qualifier of the giving itself.

10.11.title: नाशयामि — Krishna's first-person promise to destroy darkness
10.11.body: नाशयामि is the causative (णिच्) of √नश्, लट्, उत्तम एकवचन — 'I cause to perish'. Object is तमः, qualified by अज्ञानजम् ('born of ignorance', a tatpuruṣa). The instrument is the locative-feeling तृतीया ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता ('with the radiant lamp of knowledge').

10.11.title: आत्मभावस्थः — abiding in His own nature
10.11.body: आत्मभावस्थः is a bahuvrīhi adjective qualifying अहम् — 'I, abiding in My own state'. The verse rejects the idea that Krishna leaves His own ground to destroy ignorance; the destruction happens from within His unchanged seat. अनुकम्पार्थम् ('for the sake of compassion') is a -अर्थम् compound used adverbially in द्वितीया एकवचन — the standard Sanskrit way of expressing purpose.

10.12.title: परं ब्रह्म परं धाम — Arjuna's hymn begins
10.12.body: Arjuna addresses Krishna with a series of predicate phrases in प्रथमा एकवचन — परं ब्रह्म, परं धाम, पवित्रं परमं, with भवान् (the honorific 'you') as subject. The repetition of परम् as an adjective hammers the supremacy claim into each epithet.

10.12.title: Six द्वितीया epithets for Him
10.12.body: पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यम् आदिदेवम् अजं विभुम् — six द्वितीया एकवचन adjectives. The case is द्वितीया because they are objects of आहुः ('they call you') which arrives in 10.13. Sanskrit happily lets a hymn's grammar span across verses.

10.13.title: आहुः — the finite verb that gathers verse 12
10.13.body: आहुः (√ब्रू/√अह्, लिट्, प्रथम बहुवचन — 'they have said') is the long-awaited anchor. Subjects are ऋषयः सर्वे, नारदः (in apposition with देवर्षिः), असितः, देवलः, व्यासः — all प्रथमा एकवचन or बहुवचन. The accumulated द्वितीया epithets of 10.12 are its object.

10.13.title: स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे — Arjuna's clinching evidence
10.13.body: ब्रवीषि (√ब्रू, लट्, मध्यम एकवचन — 'you yourself say'). The point is rhetorical: the seers say it, and you confirm it to me (मे, चतुर्थी/षष्ठी of advantage). स्वयम् ('yourself') makes Krishna His own witness, a logically inescapable position.

10.14.title: ऋतं मन्ये — Arjuna's first-person assent
10.14.body: मन्ये is √मन्, लट् आत्मनेपद, उत्तम एकवचन ('I consider'). Object is सर्वम् एतत् qualified by ऋतम् ('true') — सामानाधिकरण्य holds in नपुंसकलिंग एकवचन across the three words. यन्मां वदसि is a relative clause expanding एतत्.

10.14.title: न देवाः न दानवाः — the two negated subjects
10.14.body: विदुः is √विद्, लट्, प्रथम बहुवचन ('they know'), with subjects देवाः and दानवाः both negated. ते (षष्ठी) qualifies व्यक्तिम् ('your manifestation', द्वितीया एकवचन). भगवन् is सम्बोधन — vocative, not a participant in the action.

10.15.title: आत्मना आत्मानम् — the reflexive self-knowing
10.15.body: वेत्थ is √विद्, लट्, मध्यम एकवचन ('you know'). The doubled आत्मन् — instrumental आत्मना and accusative आत्मानम् — captures self-knowledge as a closed reflexive loop: 'by yourself you know yourself'. स्वयम् एव intensifies the reflexivity.

10.15.title: Five सम्बोधन epithets in a row
10.15.body: पुरुषोत्तम, भूतभावन, भूतेश, देवदेव, जगत्पते — five vocatives in succession. सम्बोधन never participates in the action; here they are pure honorific apostrophe, and the case ending alone (no kāraka role) is what tells us so.

10.16.title: अर्हसि वक्तुम् — the auxiliary + infinitive politeness
10.16.body: वक्तुम् is the तुमुन् infinitive of √वच् ('to speak'); अर्हसि (√अर्ह्, लट्, मध्यम एकवचन — 'you are worthy / are obliged') is the polite request marker. अशेषेण ('without remainder', तृतीया) is the manner adverb — Arjuna asks for the complete list.

10.16.title: व्याप्य तिष्ठसि — ल्यबन्त plus finite
10.16.body: व्याप्य is the ल्यबन्त of वि+√आप् ('having pervaded') — because the root has a prefix, the absolutive suffix is ल्यप् (-य), not क्त्वा (-त्वा). तिष्ठसि is √स्था, लट्, मध्यम एकवचन ('you stand'). The construction is 'having pervaded, you stand' — Krishna pervades and remains, simultaneous and not sequential despite the absolutive's normal sequencing force.

10.17.title: कथं विद्याम् — the विधिलिङ् of inquiry
10.17.body: विद्याम् is √विद्, विधिलिङ्, उत्तम एकवचन — 'how should I know?' The विधिलिङ् ending -याम् marks the deliberative mood: Arjuna is not asking for a fact but for a method. परिचिन्तयन् is the शतृ participle ('continuously contemplating') agreeing with the implicit अहम्.

10.17.title: केषु केषु भावेषु — the सप्तमी of contemplation
10.17.body: केषु केषु is reduplicated interrogative in सप्तमी बहुवचन ('in which and which states?'), with भावेषु in the same case. चिन्त्यः is the kṛtya / gerundive of √चिन्त् ('to be contemplated'), and मया is the तृतीया agent — the classic gerundive construction.

10.18.title: तृप्तिः न अस्ति — the negated existence of satisfaction
10.18.body: तृप्तिः ('satisfaction') is प्रथमा एकवचन subject of the elided अस्ति negated by न. मे is षष्ठी ('mine'). शृण्वतः is the genitive present participle of √श्रु ('of the one listening') agreeing with मे — Arjuna casts himself as a listener whose hearing has no terminus.

10.18.title: अमृतम् — the verse's last word as judgment
10.18.body: Arjuna ends the request with अमृतम् ('nectar') in apposition to the implied 'this speech'. The single word valorizes everything that has been said and demands more — Krishna's words are not merely true (10.14's ऋतम्) but immortalizing.

10.19.title: हन्त — the affectionate particle of consent
10.19.body: हन्त is an indeclinable expressing affectionate agreement ('alas/well-then/so be it'). कथयिष्यामि is √कथ् (denominative), लृट्, उत्तम एकवचन — spotted by the -ष्य- infix. Krishna agrees to enumerate but immediately warns: नास्ति अन्तः ('there is no end').

10.19.title: प्राधान्यतः — only the principal ones
10.19.body: प्राधान्यतः is an adverb in -तस् ('according to prominence'), framing the entire enumeration as selective. विस्तरस्य is षष्ठी एकवचन ('of the extent') with अन्तः ('end') as its head — the syntactic admission that the list is necessarily incomplete.

10.20.title: अहम् आत्मा — the first identification
10.20.body: Krishna opens the enumeration not with an external glory but with अहम् आत्मा ('I am the self') and the locative qualifier सर्वभूताशयस्थितः ('seated in the heart of all beings', a tatpuruṣa-bahuvrīhi chain). The order matters: inner identification precedes outer enumeration.

10.20.title: आदिः मध्यं अन्तः — beginning, middle, end
10.20.body: अहम् आदिः च मध्यं च अन्तः एव च — three predicates linked by च and emphasized by एव. भूतानाम् is the षष्ठी बहुवचन under which all three nominatives belong. The triplet is a stock device for total temporal coverage.

10.21.title: आदित्यानां विष्णुः — the षष्ठी-template begins
10.21.body: From here the pattern is fixed: षष्ठी बहुवचन (the class) + प्रथमा एकवचन (the supreme member). आदित्यानां विष्णुः, ज्योतिषां रविः अंशुमान् ('the radiant sun'), मरुतां मरीचिः, नक्षत्राणां शशी — four pairs in one verse.

10.21.title: अंशुमान् — the suffix that means 'possessed of rays'
10.21.body: अंशुमान् is the सूर्य's epithet formed with the matup suffix on अंशु ('ray') — 'one possessing rays'. The suffix yields प्रथमा एकवचन पुल्लिंग, agreeing with रविः in apposition. Vibhūti enumeration loves these descriptive epithets that capture each entity's defining property.

10.22.title: अस्मि — the explicit copula returns
10.22.body: After 10.21's verbless ascriptions, अस्मि (√अस्, लट्, उत्तम एकवचन) now appears repeatedly as the copula. वेदानां सामवेदः अस्मि — among the four Vedas, the Sāmaveda is chosen for its musicality, an aesthetic preference within the canon itself.

10.22.title: इन्द्रियाणां मनः — mind is one of the eleven
10.22.body: इन्द्रियाणाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन) gathers the senses, and मनः is named as the supreme — in Sāṅkhya enumeration the mind is the eleventh indriya, the inner coordinator. भूतानाम् (of beings) gets चेतना ('consciousness') — life is named as life's essence.

10.23.title: रुद्राणां शङ्करः — Krishna naming Śiva as Himself
10.23.body: Among the eleven Rudras, Krishna names शङ्करः (Śiva) as His vibhūti — the verse refuses any sectarian division. वित्तेशः ('lord of wealth', Kubera) for यक्ष-रक्षस् (षष्ठी बहुवचन), पावकः (Agni) for वसु, मेरुः for शिखरिणाम् ('among peaked ones').

10.23.title: शिखरिणाम् — the matup-suffix substantivized
10.23.body: शिखरिणाम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन of शिखरिन् ('possessing a peak'), a matup-style derivative of शिखर ('peak') functioning as a noun for 'peaked mountains'. Sanskrit constantly converts adjectives into noun-classes via case alone, and the vibhūti chapter exploits this.

10.24.title: विद्धि — the लोट् of pedagogical instruction
10.24.body: विद्धि is √विद्, लोट्, मध्यम एकवचन — 'know!' Object is मां qualified by मुख्यं ('chief') and बृहस्पतिम् in apposition, all in द्वितीया. The imperative inserts a moment of direct instruction into the enumeration.

10.24.title: स्कन्दः and सागरः — war-leader and water-leader
10.24.body: सेनानीनाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'among generals') gets स्कन्दः; सरसाम् ('among waters/lakes') gets सागरः ('the ocean'). The pattern is fixed: each domain's apex is Krishna, and the apex is chosen by qualitative supremacy, not numerical first-ness.

10.25.title: गिरामेकमक्षरम् — the one syllable
10.25.body: गिराम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन of गिर् ('word/speech'); the supreme is एकम् अक्षरम् ('the one syllable') — Oṃ, named obliquely. The use of एकम् with अक्षरम् (literally 'the imperishable') doubles as 'one syllable' and 'the indestructible one'.

10.25.title: जपयज्ञः and हिमालयः — quiet supremes
10.25.body: Among यज्ञ Krishna chooses not the grand ritual but जपयज्ञः (the silent repetition); among स्थावराणाम् ('among stationary things') the हिमालयः. The verse repeatedly prefers what is silent, single, or motionless — vibhūti is not always loud.

10.26.title: अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणाम् — the peepal tree
10.26.body: Verbless ascription: अश्वत्थः (प्रथमा एकवचन) is named over सर्ववृक्षाणाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of all trees'). The अश्वत्थ is the same tree that opens Gītā 15 as ऊर्ध्वमूलम् — a deep cross-reference woven into the vibhūti list.

10.26.title: Four classes, four supremes
10.26.body: देवर्षीणां नारदः, गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः, सिद्धानां कपिलः मुनिः — three classes of semi-divine beings each get their named exemplar. The repetition of च across the verse is the connective that keeps the list a single grammatical structure.

10.27.title: विद्धि माम् — second imperative of instruction
10.27.body: विद्धि (लोट्) recurs, again with मां as object, qualified by उच्चैःश्रवसम् ('the high-eared one', द्वितीया) and the apposition अमृतोद्भवम् ('born from the nectar-churning'). The kṛdanta उद्भव specifies origin in the churning event.

10.27.title: नराधिपः — the king among men
10.27.body: नराणाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of men') gets नराधिपम् ('lord-of-men', द्वितीया एकवचन) — the king. The case is द्वितीया because it continues as object of विद्धि. ऐरावतम् for गजेन्द्राणाम् completes the elephant–horse–king triad.

10.28.title: वज्रम् among weapons, कामधुक् among cows
10.28.body: आयुधानाम् gets the वज्र (Indra's thunderbolt); धेनूनाम् gets कामधुक् ('the wish-yielder', the magical cow). कामधुक् is an उपपद-तत्पुरुष कृदन्त — काम (object) + √दुह् ('to milk') + क्विप् → 'one who milks desires'.

10.28.title: कन्दर्पः and वासुकिः — the chief of love and serpent
10.28.body: प्रजनः ('procreation') is identified with कन्दर्पः (Kāmadeva); सर्पाणाम् gets वासुकिः. Vibhūti enumeration is morally neutral: Krishna names the god of erotic desire alongside cosmic snakes alongside Vedic Indra without flinching.

10.29.title: नागानाम् vs सर्पाणाम् — two snake classes
10.29.body: 10.28 named वासुकिः among सर्पाणाम्; 10.29 names अनन्तः among नागानाम्. Sanskrit distinguishes the two — सर्प are venomous serpents, नाग are the larger semi-divine serpents — and Krishna gives a different exemplar to each class.

10.29.title: यमः संयमतां — the lord of self-restrainers
10.29.body: संयमताम् is the शतृ participle of √यम् with सं ('of those self-restraining'), षष्ठी बहुवचन. The supreme is यमः — the god of death is here cast not as terror but as the perfect self-restrainer, the etymological root of his name made glory.

10.30.title: प्रह्लादः among दैत्यानाम्
10.30.body: Among the अsuras, Krishna names not the strongest demon but प्रह्लादः — Hiraṇyakaśipu's son, the bhakta who survived his father's persecution. The verse encodes a theological move: even within the demonic lineage, the bhakta is the supreme expression of Krishna.

10.30.title: कालः कलयताम् — time of the time-counters
10.30.body: कलयताम् is the शतृ participle of √कल् ('to reckon/count'), षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of those who measure'. The supreme measurer is कालः (time) itself — a recursive identification where the measure becomes the measurer. The same etymology returns in Gītā 11.32.

10.31.title: पवनः पवताम् — the wind among purifiers
10.31.body: पवताम् is the शतृ participle of √पू ('to purify'), षष्ठी बहुवचन; the supreme is पवनः ('wind') — same root, different derivation. Sanskrit lets you make the etymological pun structural: the wind purifies, and the wind is named the supreme purifier.

10.31.title: रामः शस्त्रभृताम् — the wielder of weapons
10.31.body: शस्त्रभृताम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन of शस्त्रभृत् ('weapon-bearer', a तत्पुरुष). रामः is named — Daśaratha's son. The verse stages a quiet self-reference: Vāsudeva-Kṛṣṇa names His own previous avatāra as His vibhūti, blurring the inside/outside of the speaker.

10.32.title: आदिः अन्तः मध्यम् — the triad returns
10.32.body: Echoing 10.20's आदिः-मध्यम्-अन्तः for भूतानाम्, here the same triad is applied to सर्गाणाम् ('of creations', षष्ठी बहुवचन). The repetition is structural — the chapter is folded in on itself, with the same temporal claim made first for beings, now for cosmoi.

10.32.title: अध्यात्मविद्या and वादः — knowledge and disputation
10.32.body: विद्यानाम् gets अध्यात्मविद्या (the science of the inner Self); प्रवदताम् ('of those who debate', शतृ षष्ठी of √वद् with प्र) gets वादः ('honest debate' — distinct from जल्प and वितण्डा in Nyāya). Even within disputation, the truth-seeking variety is named His vibhūti.

10.33.title: अक्षराणाम् अकारः — the letter A
10.33.body: Among letters, the अकार ('the letter A') — the first vowel, the unspoken substrate of every consonant. The verse picks the simplest possible exemplar to make a structural point: not the most ornate but the most foundational is His.

10.33.title: द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य — the compound class
10.33.body: सामासिकस्य is षष्ठी एकवचन ('of the compound-type') — Pāṇinian grammatical category. Among the four compound types (तत्पुरुष, बहुव्रीहि, द्वन्द्व, अव्ययीभाव), Krishna picks द्वन्द्व — the equal-pairing compound — because it gives both members nominative-equal weight.

10.34.title: मृत्युः सर्वहरः — death the all-taker
10.34.body: अहम् मृत्युः सर्वहरः — Krishna identifies as death itself, qualified by the तत्पुरुष सर्वहरः ('taker of all'). The same Krishna who just named प्रह्लाद and रामः now names death. उद्भवः भविष्यताम् ('origination of things-to-come') balances death with birth, both as His.

10.34.title: Seven feminine qualities — कीर्तिः श्रीः वाक् etc.
10.34.body: कीर्तिः, श्रीः, वाक्, स्मृतिः, मेधा, धृतिः, क्षमा — seven feminine nouns in प्रथमा एकवचन, gathered under नारीणाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'among women / feminine things'). Each is both a quality and (in mythology) a goddess; the grammar lets both readings live at once.

10.35.title: बृहत्साम — the chief of Sāmans
10.35.body: साम्नाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of Sāman chants') gets the बृहत्साम; छन्दसाम् ('of metres') gets गायत्री. The verse picks within each Vedic subcategory the most cosmic exemplar — and gāyatrī is also (independently) the meter of supreme mantra status.

10.35.title: मासानां मार्गशीर्षः — the month of harvest-beginning
10.35.body: Among months, मार्गशीर्षः (Nov–Dec) is named — the month marking the start of the cooler, fruitful period in the lunar calendar. ऋतूनां कुसुमाकरः ('the flower-mine', i.e., spring) parallels it for seasons: each cycle's most generative phase is His.

10.36.title: द्यूतम् छलयताम् — gambling among the deceitful
10.36.body: छलयताम् is the शतृ participle of छल् ('to deceive'), षष्ठी बहुवचन; द्यूतम् ('gambling') is named — and this is the same gambling that lost Yudhiṣṭhira his kingdom. The vibhūti list is unflinching: even what destroyed the Pāṇḍava cause is named His.

10.36.title: जयः व्यवसायः सत्त्वम् — three abstract supremes
10.36.body: जयः ('victory'), व्यवसायः ('resolve/effort'), सत्त्वम् ('strength') — three abstract qualities in प्रथमा एकवचन, each predicated as Krishna. सत्त्ववताम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन of सत्त्ववत्) — 'of the strong-ones' — repeats the matup-suffix substantivization seen earlier.

10.37.title: वासुदेवः among the वृष्णीनाम् — Krishna naming Himself
10.37.body: वृष्णीनाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — the Vṛṣṇi clan) gets वासुदेवः — Krishna's own clan-name. The verse openly self-identifies. पाण्डवानाम् (the audience's clan) gets धनञ्जयः — Arjuna himself. Speaker and listener are both folded into the enumeration.

10.37.title: व्यासः and उशनाः — sage and seer-poet
10.37.body: मुनीनाम् gets व्यासः (the author of this very text, recursively); कवीनाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of poets/seers') gets उशनाः कविः (Śukrācārya). The genealogical loop is complete: Krishna is the author of the text in which He names His own authorship.

10.38.title: दण्डः दमयताम् — the rod of disciplinarians
10.38.body: दमयताम् is the शतृ participle of the causative of √दम् ('to subdue'), षष्ठी बहुवचन; the supreme is दण्डः ('the rod, punishment'). नीतिः जिगीषताम् ('policy of those wishing to conquer', desiderative of √जि) — both governance tools are claimed.

10.38.title: मौनम् गुह्यानाम् — silence among secrets
10.38.body: गुह्यानाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन — 'of secrets') gets मौनम् ('silence'). The vibhūti's deepest secret is its non-utterance — a paradox the chapter signals openly. ज्ञानवताम् (matup again) gets ज्ञानम् itself, the recursive form characteristic of this list.

10.39.title: बीजम् — the seed of all beings
10.39.body: यच्चापि बीजम् — relative clause, with बीजम् in प्रथमा एकवचन नपुंसकलिंग; तत् अहम् identifies the seed as Krishna. The verse abstracts from particular exemplars to the principle: He is whatever functions as origin-cause in any being whatsoever.

10.39.title: न तत् अस्ति विना यत्स्यात् — the negation of independent being
10.39.body: विना ('without') governs मया (तृतीया) — 'apart from Me'. यत्स्यात् is the विधिलिङ् of √अस् in a hypothetical clause ('which might exist'). चराचरम् ('moving and unmoving') is the universal scope. No being holds existence independently of Him.

10.40.title: न अन्तः अस्ति — explicit admission of the list's open end
10.40.body: अन्तः ('end') is प्रथमा एकवचन; the elided अस्ति is negated by न. विभूतीनाम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन) is the head. The verse echoes 10.19's नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे — bracketing the enumeration with the same admission of incompleteness.

10.40.title: उद्देशतः — the adverb of representativeness
10.40.body: उद्देशतः ('representatively, indicatively') is a -तस् adverb. प्रोक्तः (past passive participle of √वच् with प्र) is masculine प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with विस्तरः. The list was indicative, not exhaustive — and the chapter's framing has held this position since 10.19.

10.41.title: यद्यद्विभूतिमत् — the doubled relative
10.41.body: यद्यत् is the reduplicated relative pronoun ('whatsoever'); विभूतिमत् is matup-suffixed adjective ('possessing vibhūti'). The doubling generalizes — any case at all is being claimed. The triplet विभूतिमत्, श्रीमत्, ऊर्जितम् gathers three near-synonyms of excellence.

10.41.title: तेजोंऽशसम्भवम् — born of a fragment of My splendour
10.41.body: तत्तत् ('that, that') correlates with यद्यत्. The predicate is तेजोंशसम्भवम् — a compound 'arising from a portion (अंश) of My splendour (तेजस्)'. The formula generalizes the list: don't memorize items, recognize the pattern of partial manifestation.

10.42.title: बहुना एतेन ज्ञातेन किम् — the dismissive question
10.42.body: बहुना एतेन ज्ञातेन is तृतीया एकवचन with the past passive participle ज्ञातेन ('by this much being known'); किम् ('what use?'). Krishna dismisses the entire enumeration just completed — a rhetorical move that resets the chapter onto a single sealing statement.

10.42.title: एकांशेन स्थितः — the chapter's final seal
10.42.body: विष्टभ्य is the ल्यबन्त of वि+√स्तम्भ् ('having supported/upheld') — the prefix वि- forces ल्यप् (-य), not क्त्वा. इदं कृत्स्नं जगत् ('this whole world') is the द्वितीया object. एकांशेन (तृतीया एकवचन — 'with a single fragment') is the instrument. The vibhūti list is reframed: He pervades not by accumulation of glories but by a single fragment of Himself.
